mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate labor income, which drives consumption and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of months, or mean return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9% indicates persistent slack in the labor market that depresses consumer spending expectations and triggers risk-off positioning, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 9%, or the mean forward return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both the federal funds rate and the CPI index signal a restrictive real monetary stance that tightens financial conditions and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month increase in both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) in a forward-return regression.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of a housing-led recession, month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal stabilization of the construction sector and broader credit markets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations during 2009-2012, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over that period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects capacity utilization gains and operating leverage that expand corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or rank correlation between INDPRO change and forward returns is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% traps the yield curve near the zero lower bound, compressing net interest margins for financial stocks and signaling deflation risk, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as volatility rises.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirm broad-based demand recovery that outpaces supply constraints, lifting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month increase in both RSAFS and HOUST
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-HOUST signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in predicting forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand momentum that leads investors to upgrade baseline corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations, or the rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03.
